sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cagliari have shown decent form in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins, including a strong 4-0 home victory against Hellas Verona, but also suffering 2 losses. Lecce, on the other hand, have struggled, managing only 1 win in their last 5 games with a recent 2-1 home win over Udinese, but losing 3 matches, indicating defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 encounters, Cagliari have dominated Lecce, winning both matches with scores of 4-1 at home and 2-1 away. These results suggest Cagliari have a psychological edge, especially at the Unipol Domus, where they previously thrashed Lecce.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Cagliari, sitting 13th with 28 points, have scored 28 goals but underperformed their expected goals by -5.23, ranking poorly in attack creation (17th in big chances created). Lecce, in 18th with 21 points, have struggled offensively, scoring only 15 goals all season, and their defense has conceded 31, highlighting their relegation battle.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Cagliari will rely on forwards like Semih Kılıçsoy (4 goals) and Sebastiano Esposito (3 goals, 4 assists), though they miss key midfielders like Gianluca Gaetano. Lecce's attack looks blunt without Lameck Banda (3 goals), with Nikola Štulić (2 goals) needing to step up amidst their limited firepower.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Cagliari's ability to score late (24% of goals in 76-90 minutes) could exploit Lecce's tendency to concede in the same period (19% of goals conceded). Lecce’s poor final third efficiency (low penalty area entries) against Cagliari’s high foul rate (2nd most in the league) may lead to a scrappy, card-heavy game.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Cagliari’s home advantage, recent form, and head-to-head dominance, they are favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 60% home win probability. Lecce’s struggles in attack and poor away form (4 losses in last 5) make an upset unlikely, though a draw remains possible if they tighten defensively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains goalless between Cagliari and Lecce at the Unipol Domus. Neither team has managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments in the attacking third. Cagliari have dominated possession and created more threatening opportunities, while Lecce have struggled to convert their entries into dangerous chances. Both teams have shown resilience defensively, with no major errors leading to goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Cagliari's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Cagliari have been the more dominant side in terms of ball control and territorial advantage. They have consistently entered the final third and penetrated the penalty area with relative ease. Despite creating a notable big chance, they failed to capitalize, missing a clear opportunity to take the lead. Their aerial dominance and balanced duels suggest they are physically imposing, which could play a key role later. However, their lack of shots on target is a concern that needs addressing in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Lecce's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Lecce have been on the back foot for much of the first half, with less possession and fewer dangerous moments compared to Cagliari. They have managed to match Cagliari in terms of final third entries and penalty area presence, but their inability to create big chances highlights a lack of cutting edge in attack. Defensively, they have held firm, though their lower aerial duel success rate could be exploited. Their single shot on target shows a glimmer of attacking intent, but they need more creativity to turn the game in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Cagliari start with significant attacking momentum, controlling the early stages with sustained pressure in the opponent’s half. Their attacking spells were prominent in the opening minutes and again towards the end of the half, pushing Lecce onto the defensive. Lecce, however, found their rhythm midway through the half, with a notable shift in momentum as they launched several attacking forays. Their pressure peaked during a prolonged period in the middle of the half, though it waned as Cagliari regained control in the closing stages.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Cagliari, Leonardo Pavoletti stands out as a potential game-changer in the second half, given his involvement in key moments despite missing a big chance. His physical presence could be crucial in breaking down Lecce’s defense. Gabriele Zappa also showed promise with creative contributions that might lead to a breakthrough. On Lecce’s side, Walid Cheddira looks to be their most likely source of a goal, having already tested the Cagliari defense with a shot on target. His ability to capitalize on limited chances could be vital for Lecce’s hopes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Cagliari continue their dominance in possession and push for a goal, leveraging their home advantage and physical edge. They will need to be more clinical in front of goal to convert their territorial control into a lead. Lecce, on the other hand, might adopt a counter-attacking approach, looking to exploit any gaps left by Cagliari’s forward push. Defensive solidity will be crucial for both sides, but Cagliari appear better positioned to edge this contest if they can sharpen their finishing.
